Dear Friend, Before we talk about our annual summit, this update was simply too important not to share.

Over the weekend, the **South Carolina Freedom Caucus** led the way in calling out the radical Left for spewing vicious attacks after Charlie Kirk’s tragic assassination. After SCFC lawmakers mounted a viral public pressure campaign – leading to statements by Members of Congress and even [President Trump ](http://give.statefreedomcaucus.org/ls/click?upn=u001.SSCAglCEkhp55PDWKhWTjoyqjO-2F7Lw7RiV-2F-2FO65-2FN-2BvyjO6fschN-2FAY-2Ftf2q4PyijFICpWUeoNRaG4IqTqgyf8fYarqTUu-2By3w8yMnLNGMQ-3DBglm_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVBZyx1SQGGYhOdwcjZYtHerCB9e9GkvlERi-2BP0TuzsecQJ2tSQaAPrY5c3QnK4QlCd7WzAhJDsmCXA1fXmX2RTJyj1G-2FI2a7-2FurePhDGvnZdwK0TbwaPslDp1Fri99APVBwjjYVnSuFFMNC0myfAh-2B7npTly-2B-2FLriOJhFv-2FrXANJuPP3bY6jQvXbvmsxgdAQit32NoMGedskMQ8-2BHqixCIw8j2f24obnWnvEp8nv8elmZoN8cisl2Cg-2FUDfuV8N18w-3D)– Clemson University finally caved and [fired](http://give.statefreedomcaucus.org/ls/click?upn=u001.SSCAglCEkhp55PDWKhWTjuIhXwy38OQ8irfHG-2F7Xc57p-2F9TfzwAoUwEnPLuevz41xfnaAlN6w0ilkHAscH-2F-2FUg-3D-3DEzYB_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVBZyx1SQGGYhOdwcjZYtHerCB9e9GkvlERi-2BP0TuzsecQJ2tSQaAPrY5c3QnK4QlCd7WzAhJDsmCXA1fXmX2RTJyj1G-2FI2a7-2FurePhDGvnZdyPoxs2ijP8257eZ-2FYLGfBmAyHSDqwIFfqDKrh-2Frfx2FVGjD6dlORr8SPzcQuS2cVhg0-2F5XZcuvPxxzboofGpiZomWz4pV4bIpZhZohm34PaSB0ldiMW6mu79kAGoATcKr6fP1xRNLpyQ7lLlH-2FOBcg-3D) the three professors who celebrated Kirk’s horrific murder.We couldn’t more proud of the absolute master class our SCFC lawmakers demonstrated in getting Clemson to remove these woke liberals from their taxpayer-funded jobs. We’ll be highlighting more victories next week as our bold states continue to make stands like this across the country.
---
|
---
**Recapping our 2025 Annual Summit!** Along with donors, grassroots leaders, and conservative thought leaders, over 100 state lawmakers from our 13 state freedom caucuses came to Dallas, Texas, earlier this month for our **2nd Annual State Freedom Caucus Summit**. Our lawmakers assembled to network and strategize with each other, celebrate their successes, and most importantly, to be recognized for their amazing victories this past year.After hearing from U.S. Border Czar Tom Homan and Florida Governor Ron DeSantis at our opening ceremony the the night before, the members came together for our Awards Dinner, emceed by **The Blaze's Alex Stein**. We gave out 9 awards to honor their grit and determination in advancing our shared conservative values in their respective state capitols. It was a very memorable night!Here were the winners:
---
|
---
**Pettiest Punishment Award** This award is given to the member or state freedom caucus that fought for conservative principles...and got punished by the Swamp for their efforts.This year, **Georgia Freedom Caucus's State Senator Colton Moore** won the award for calling out the corrupt former Speaker of the House. Subsequently, the current "Republican" Speaker banned Colton from the House chamber. When the governor gave his annual State of the State address in the House chamber, Colton was determined to attend, despite the ban, because it was his constitutional duty to represent his voters. In response, the sergeant-at-arms prevented him from attending and the Speaker's attorney physically assaulted Colton, pushing him down onto the ground. Colton -- and not the attorney -- was arrested and booked in the same Fulton County jail as President Trump was several months before!
---
---
But Colton got the last laugh. After immense pressure from the grassroots, the Lieutenant Governor, the State Republican Party, and even the State Democrat Party, the charges against him were dropped and the ban was lifted.Here's Colton accepting the award along with the award presenters, **Missouri Freedom Caucus's State Senator Nick Schroer and State Rep. Justin Sparks**. (Yes, the actual award was a fraternity paddle)
---
---
|
---
**Best In Session Fight Award** This award goes to the state freedom caucus that waged the best fight while the legislature was in session. The **Illinois Freedom Caucus** won for defeating a bill that was an Illinois Democrat priority this year. The bill would have cracked down on homeschooling by imposing registration, home and curriculum inspections, and even vaccine oversight—all while their own public schools continued to fail. The Illinois Freedom Caucus coordinated with homeschool advocates to flood the legislature with over 75,000 witness slips, organize rallies, and deliver sharp committee questioning and testimony. Thanks to this massive grassroots pushback, especially from homeschool families in Democrat districts, the bill was defeated. An excellent policy victory while in the super-minority in the legislature.
---
|
---
**Best Out of Session Fight Award** This award goes to the state freedom caucus that kept on fighting even after the legislature gaveled out of session.The winner, the **Montana Freedom Caucus** , won because it executed an effective communications strategy to label 9 RINO Republican Senators as “The Nasty 9.” These nasty Senators conspired with Democrats throughout the session to kill conservative bills and grow government. The constant pressure on "The Nasty 9" by the Montana Freedom Caucus led to their censure by the Montana Republican Party, and will be targeted by conservatives next election cycle.
---
|
---
**Rookie of the Year Award** This award is given to the newly-elected lawmaker who hit the ground running, pushed hard for freedom-loving principles, and was an integral part of their state freedom caucus. The winner was **Oklahoma Freedom Caucus's Julie McIntosh** \-- a freshman Senator, who came on board early in the session, and got right to work. She quickly became a respected cornerstone of the OK Freedom Caucus, the Oklahoma State Senate, and a draw to her peers for her policy chops, toughness, and values. Here she is with award presenters, Steve Cowles and Scott Parkinson:
---
---
|
---
**Best Communications Award** This award goes to the state freedom caucus that loudly champions its conservative priorities through all means necessary - press conferences, TV and print interviews, social media posts, and email newsletters.Winning the award were the amazing men and women of the **Maryland Freedom Caucus**. For the past decade, the quote “Republican” message coming out of Maryland was the voice of RINO Larry Hogan. Starting in 2025, the authentic voice of conservatives became the Maryland Freedom Caucus. On the floor, in the press, podcasts, live stream videos, social media, op-eds, letters to the editor, television–they lived rent free in the heads of their squishy colleagues and pretender-to-the-throne Governor Wes Moore. Relentless on green energy scams, fiscal discipline, and anti-corruption, they have set a new standard in the state.
---
|
---
**Best Floor Speech Award** This self-explanatory award went to the **Louisiana Freedom Caucus's Beryl Amedee** , who gave a courageous speech on the dangers of destructive DEI policies in the Bayou State. Watch it here:
---
[](http://give.statefreedomcaucus.org/ls/click?upn=u001.SSCAglCEkhp55PDWKhWTjr88r-2FUR-2FKWzr-2FxV7Zxi8K73bBjZxgvAd5P9nparh-2BSIDRnHKBcuyeEfqLxOMQwten1KF7OFxLbpmwFlcUqE0HE-3DpkVv_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVBZyx1SQGGYhOdwcjZYtHerCB9e9GkvlERi-2BP0TuzsecQJ2tSQaAPrY5c3QnK4QlCd7WzAhJDsmCXA1fXmX2RTJyj1G-2FI2a7-2FurePhDGvnZd3xhmQ3n5n-2BRMPKqRzvM-2FeZxnES-2FTOTv8jTpKwfer6BrnuDSlV3hC3zIzoarBXAekdvpEuxIegHl5I15k0Lu110ErB6uovJI6vmxYi8gDgwounVXp97FaX5ggR9wzWcfN-2Bf4gJWDVOc1rZYTVeXNP1c-3D)
---
|
---
**Best State Director Award** The Network has a state director in each of our states. These are the brave men and women who work with the state freedom caucuses, other conservative lawmakers, the grassroots, and subject-matter experts. This year's winner was **Maria Nate** , our Idaho state director. She was targeted by so-called Republicans inside and out of the legislature, the liberal Speaker of the state house, and other nefarious forces who hated that she strongly stood on her principles to advance a freedom-loving agenda.
---
|
---
**Best State Lawmaker Award** Freedom Caucus Members do not merely sit in rooms with each other and challenge one another to be more conservative. They listen, adapt, suggest action, build consensus, lead by example, and jump in front of the slings and arrows that inevitably come for the group as a whole. This year's winner was **South Carolina's State Rep. April Cromer** , who has not only demonstrated these qualities, but has refined them and grown as a leader within the **South Carolina Freedom Caucus**. In one instance, she stood at the podium in the state house for 90 long minutes to rail against the state budget that was chocked full of egregious spending and absent of any strong conservative reforms.
---
|
---
**Best State Freedom Caucus Award** Then there is the one award to rule them all! This award isn't about notching the most victories or killing the most big government bills. Instead, the winner is the caucus that most exemplifies what it means to be an infantry unit of conservative warriors. They vote together, plan together, defend each other, and they loudly explain their intentions to the public. And most importantly, they pray together as brothers and sisters who respect and cherish each others' contributions to the fight.This year's winner was the **Wyoming Freedom Caucus**. Just a mere two and a half years ago, they only had eight members. But because they demonstrated a courageous, unified ability to work together, they quickly doubled in size to sixteen. Then they swept their elections last year and took control of the Wyoming House. The speaker, the pro tem, and other positions of power are all held by Wyoming Freedom Caucus members. Policies like school choice, property tax cuts, and a ban on transgender surgeries for minors were blocked in previous years because the Democrats and liberal Republicans were in charge. But those issues all passed into law once the Wyoming Freedom Caucus took control. The left-leaning local press can't stand that the Wyoming Freedom Caucus is so successful, but that hasn't stopped them from reporting on them everyday. A recent analysis shows that they have written about the WY Freedom Caucus for 900+ consecutive days. And that record still hasn't been broken. Thanks to their efforts, the Wyoming Freedom Caucus is a household name in the state!
---
---
|
---

The battles of 2026 are right around the corner, and the other side is already gearing up. **That’s why your support today is so critical.** Every gift helps us finish strong, prepare for the fights ahead, and ensure we’re ready to deliver even bigger wins in the year to come. Will you stand with us now so we can charge into 2026 with the strength we need?

clean_body: | | | | Dear Friend, Before we talk about our annual summit, this update was simply too important not to share. Over the weekend, the South Carolina Freedom Caucus led the way in calling out the radical Left for spewing vicious attacks after Charlie Kirk’s tragic assassination. After SCFC lawmakers mounted a viral public pressure campaign – leading to statements by Members of Congress and even – Clemson University finally caved and the three professors who celebrated Kirk’s horrific murder.We couldn’t more proud of the absolute master class our SCFC lawmakers demonstrated in getting Clemson to remove these woke liberals from their taxpayer-funded jobs. We’ll be highlighting more victories next week as our bold states continue to make stands like this across the country. | Recapping our 2025 Annual Summit! Along with donors, grassroots leaders, and conservative thought leaders, over 100 state lawmakers from our 13 state freedom caucuses came to Dallas, Texas, earlier this month for our 2nd Annual State Freedom Caucus Summit. Our lawmakers assembled to network and strategize with each other, celebrate their successes, and most importantly, to be recognized for their amazing victories this past year.After hearing from U.S. Border Czar Tom Homan and Florida Governor Ron DeSantis at our opening ceremony the the night before, the members came together for our Awards Dinner, emceed by The Blaze's Alex Stein. We gave out 9 awards to honor their grit and determination in advancing our shared conservative values in their respective state capitols. It was a very memorable night!Here were the winners: | Pettiest Punishment Award This award is given to the member or state freedom caucus that fought for conservative principles...and got punished by the Swamp for their efforts.This year, Georgia Freedom Caucus's State Senator Colton Moore won the award for calling out the corrupt former Speaker of the House. Subsequently, the current "Republican" Speaker banned Colton from the House chamber. When the governor gave his annual State of the State address in the House chamber, Colton was determined to attend, despite the ban, because it was his constitutional duty to represent his voters. In response, the sergeant-at-arms prevented him from attending and the Speaker's attorney physically assaulted Colton, pushing him down onto the ground. Colton -- and not the attorney -- was arrested and booked in the same Fulton County jail as President Trump was several months before! But Colton got the last laugh. After immense pressure from the grassroots, the Lieutenant Governor, the State Republican Party, and even the State Democrat Party, the charges against him were dropped and the ban was lifted.Here's Colton accepting the award along with the award presenters, Missouri Freedom Caucus's State Senator Nick Schroer and State Rep. Justin Sparks. (Yes, the actual award was a fraternity paddle) | Best In Session Fight Award This award goes to the state freedom caucus that waged the best fight while the legislature was in session. The Illinois Freedom Caucus won for defeating a bill that was an Illinois Democrat priority this year. The bill would have cracked down on homeschooling by imposing registration, home and curriculum inspections, and even vaccine oversight—all while their own public schools continued to fail. The Illinois Freedom Caucus coordinated with homeschool advocates to flood the legislature with over 75,000 witness slips, organize rallies, and deliver sharp committee questioning and testimony. Thanks to this massive grassroots pushback, especially from homeschool families in Democrat districts, the bill was defeated. An excellent policy victory while in the super-minority in the legislature. | Best Out of Session Fight Award This award goes to the state freedom caucus that kept on fighting even after the legislature gaveled out of session.The winner, the Montana Freedom Caucus , won because it executed an effective communications strategy to label 9 RINO Republican Senators as “The Nasty 9.” These nasty Senators conspired with Democrats throughout the session to kill conservative bills and grow government. The constant pressure on "The Nasty 9" by the Montana Freedom Caucus led to their censure by the Montana Republican Party, and will be targeted by conservatives next election cycle. | Rookie of the Year Award This award is given to the newly-elected lawmaker who hit the ground running, pushed hard for freedom-loving principles, and was an integral part of their state freedom caucus. The winner was Oklahoma Freedom Caucus's Julie McIntosh \-- a freshman Senator, who came on board early in the session, and got right to work. She quickly became a respected cornerstone of the OK Freedom Caucus, the Oklahoma State Senate, and a draw to her peers for her policy chops, toughness, and values. Here she is with award presenters, Steve Cowles and Scott Parkinson: | Best Communications Award This award goes to the state freedom caucus that loudly champions its conservative priorities through all means necessary - press conferences, TV and print interviews, social media posts, and email newsletters.Winning the award were the amazing men and women of the Maryland Freedom Caucus. For the past decade, the quote “Republican” message coming out of Maryland was the voice of RINO Larry Hogan. Starting in 2025, the authentic voice of conservatives became the Maryland Freedom Caucus. On the floor, in the press, podcasts, live stream videos, social media, op-eds, letters to the editor, television–they lived rent free in the heads of their squishy colleagues and pretender-to-the-throne Governor Wes Moore. Relentless on green energy scams, fiscal discipline, and anti-corruption, they have set a new standard in the state. | Best Floor Speech Award This self-explanatory award went to the Louisiana Freedom Caucus's Beryl Amedee , who gave a courageous speech on the dangers of destructive DEI policies in the Bayou State. Watch it here: | Best State Director Award The Network has a state director in each of our states. These are the brave men and women who work with the state freedom caucuses, other conservative lawmakers, the grassroots, and subject-matter experts. This year's winner was Maria Nate , our Idaho state director. She was targeted by so-called Republicans inside and out of the legislature, the liberal Speaker of the state house, and other nefarious forces who hated that she strongly stood on her principles to advance a freedom-loving agenda. | Best State Lawmaker Award Freedom Caucus Members do not merely sit in rooms with each other and challenge one another to be more conservative. They listen, adapt, suggest action, build consensus, lead by example, and jump in front of the slings and arrows that inevitably come for the group as a whole. This year's winner was South Carolina's State Rep. April Cromer , who has not only demonstrated these qualities, but has refined them and grown as a leader within the South Carolina Freedom Caucus. In one instance, she stood at the podium in the state house for 90 long minutes to rail against the state budget that was chocked full of egregious spending and absent of any strong conservative reforms. | Best State Freedom Caucus Award Then there is the one award to rule them all! This award isn't about notching the most victories or killing the most big government bills. Instead, the winner is the caucus that most exemplifies what it means to be an infantry unit of conservative warriors. They vote together, plan together, defend each other, and they loudly explain their intentions to the public. And most importantly, they pray together as brothers and sisters who respect and cherish each others' contributions to the fight.This year's winner was the Wyoming Freedom Caucus. Just a mere two and a half years ago, they only had eight members. But because they demonstrated a courageous, unified ability to work together, they quickly doubled in size to sixteen. Then they swept their elections last year and took control of the Wyoming House. The speaker, the pro tem, and other positions of power are all held by Wyoming Freedom Caucus members. Policies like school choice, property tax cuts, and a ban on transgender surgeries for minors were blocked in previous years because the Democrats and liberal Republicans were in charge. But those issues all passed into law once the Wyoming Freedom Caucus took control. The left-leaning local press can't stand that the Wyoming Freedom Caucus is so successful, but that hasn't stopped them from reporting on them everyday. A recent analysis shows that they have written about the WY Freedom Caucus for 900+ consecutive days. And that record still hasn't been broken. Thanks to their efforts, the Wyoming Freedom Caucus is a household name in the state! | ICYMI: Our fearless lawmakers and their victories have been making headlines in the press...— > READ: \- Washington Times — > READ: \- Daily Signal — > READ: \- Daily Wire | HELP US GROW We’ve accomplished so much together this year—securing key victories that no one thought possible. But as the year winds down, we can’t let up. The battles of 2026 are right around the corner, and the other side is already gearing up. That’s why your support today is so critical. Every gift helps us finish strong, prepare for the fights ahead, and ensure we’re ready to deliver even bigger wins in the year to come. Will you stand with us now so we can charge into 2026 with the strength we need? We'll be back with another update next week. Until then, please be sure to our efforts so that we can support our State Freedom Caucuses!In Liberty, Andy RothPresident State Freedom Caucus Network |

Peter,
This week has brought some exciting news out of Minnesota: Governor Tim Walz announced he’s running for re-election. If you have a moment, I'd like to share what I’ve learned about Tim from my time working with him - and why I’m so proud to support his campaign.
Tim has always been about service to others - as a National Guardsman, as a teacher, and now as governor.
Under his leadership, Minnesota has shown the country what it looks like when government actually works for people: Child poverty has gone down. Labor rights are stronger. Every student gets free breakfast and lunch at school. And the state continues to invest in education, opportunity, and freedom so that Minnesotans have what they need to live lives of their choosing.
That progress, these results, are worth defending.
Nothing is easy in politics and public service today. Donald Trump and his allies are at work to divide Americans, to erode trust in our institutions, and to dismantle the some of the very basics that families count on - from Social Security and Medicare to the freedoms we all deserve.
**The answer to division and cynicism is leadership rooted in service -and leaders like Tim Walz remind us what it looks like when government reflects our best values.**
[**That’s why I’m asking you to donate to Governor Walz’s campaign. Together, we can defend Minnesota’s progress, stand up for democracy and deliver results.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115517088/570582816/-1761713689?refcode=body&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDc0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYWVhOTMwMy02MDk1LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VxHjD8RiZcmxdLARNJwdp5AidD6n0-WV0_lVG_6QiME=)

# **A Life of Faith, A Legacy that Endures: Remembering Charlie Kirk**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/38a02b59-06c3-4f60-9dc3-926a2660ce30?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

* * *
# Turning Point USA elects Erika Kirk as new CEO
In the wake of her husband's horrific assassination, Erika Kirk now takes the head of her late husband's conservative activism organization.
Turning Point USA **[unanimously selected Erika Kirk](https://substack.com/redirect/43eaa1ef-aee0-411a-8a13-19576d17ae71?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** as the organization's new chief executive officer and board chair Thursday, less than two weeks after her husband Charlie Kirk was fatally shot during a campus speaking engagement.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/1ebae5f5-29f7-4ddf-b100-664e51b1ca88?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The conservative organization highlighted several key aspects of the leadership transition:
* **Predetermined Succession Plan** : Charlie Kirk had previously discussed with multiple executives that he wanted his wife to lead the organization in the event of his death, according to Turning Point USA's announcement, suggesting the transition followed his explicit wishes.
* **Continuity Mission** : The board emphasized that Charlie Kirk had prepared the organization to withstand significant challenges, with current board members stating he "worked tirelessly to ensure Turning Point USA was built to survive even the greatest tests" and trained leadership for such circumstances.
* **Memorial Service Plans** : A public memorial for Charlie Kirk is scheduled for Sunday at State Farm Stadium in Glendale, Arizona September 21, featuring speeches from President Donald Trump, Vice President JD Vance, Secretary of State Marco Rubio, and other prominent political figures.

Erika Kirk's appointment represents both a personal tribute to her late husband and an institutional effort to preserve the political movement he built over more than a decade.
* * *
# Report: AOC Considers White House Bid as Progressive Democrats Eye 2028
Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez is reportedly exploring a potential 2028 presidential campaign, positioning herself as the progressive movement's standard-bearer while simultaneously challenging established Democratic leadership in the Senate.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8d89484b-4b6e-446f-9d6e-ff1bde0efdc5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* The 35-year-old New York Democrat is **[building national momentum](https://substack.com/redirect/8d89484b-4b6e-446f-9d6e-ff1bde0efdc5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** for higher office, including expanded donor databases and enhanced social media operations, while touring the country alongside Sen. Bernie Sanders on his "Fighting Oligarchy" initiative.
* Political strategists close to Ocasio-Cortez suggest she may pursue the presidency regardless of her chances of securing the nomination, viewing her candidacy as essential for maintaining progressive influence within Democratic primary politics.
* Alternative plans include a potential Senate campaign that would likely target Minority Leader Chuck Schumer's seat.
* Ocasio-Cortez has invested heavily in expanding her political reach beyond her Queens and Bronx congressional district, conducting upstate New York town halls and allocating millions toward grassroots organizing efforts nationwide. However a final decision about her future goals**remains to be seen.**

The congresswoman's ambitions is a case of the Democratic Party's erratic convulsions to stay relevant after the 2024 election. Her decision between presidential and Senate ambitions could significantly reshape both the 2028 primary landscape and New York's political hierarchy, potentially setting up the most consequential intraparty challenge the state has seen in decades.
* * *
# Trump Seeks High Court Approval to Dismiss Fed Official Over Fraud Claims
The Trump administration urged the Supreme Court Thursday to reverse federal court decisions that prevented the President Donald removing Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook amid accusations of mortgage application misconduct.
* **Executive Power Dispute** : Solicitor General D. John Sauer said that **[federal judges overstepped their authority](https://substack.com/redirect/bd590bb4-fca6-4f8c-8550-72ded6427c15?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** by interfering with presidential dismissal powers, maintaining that Cook has no constitutional right to retain her central bank position and that fraud allegations provide sufficient justification under existing statutes.
* **Financial Misconduct Accusations** : Trump initiated Cook's removal in August after Federal Housing Finance Agency Director Bill Pulte alleged she provided false information on personal mortgage documents, with administration lawyers arguing this creates ethical concerns for someone who helps determine lending rates affecting millions of Americans.
* **Judicial Intervention** : Two separate federal courts have temporarily prevented Cook's dismissal, ruling she would likely win her lawsuit and deserved proper procedural protections, enabling her continued participation in monetary policy decisions including Wednesday's quarter-point interest rate reduction.

This unprecedented legal battle marks the first time a sitting president has attempted to terminate a Federal Reserve governor. Cook, who faces no criminal charges, remains in her role while awaiting the Supreme Court's decision on whether Trump can proceed with her termination.

Judicial Intervention : Two separate federal courts have temporarily prevented Cook's dismissal, ruling she would likely win her lawsuit and deserved proper procedural protections, enabling her continued participation in monetary policy decisions including Wednesday's quarter-point interest rate reduction. This unprecedented legal battle marks the first time a sitting president has attempted to terminate a Federal Reserve governor. Cook, who faces no criminal charges, remains in her role while awaiting the Supreme Court's decision on whether Trump can proceed with her termination. You're currently a free subscriber to . subject: We may lose this election
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Yes on 50, The Election Rigging Response Act, Governor Newsom’s Ballot Measure Committee
body:
This is when we need you most.

**$3.**
Tomorrow at midnight, we close the books on the first official fundraising deadline of this campaign.
When it ends, we're legally required to report what we've raised. We'll make the number of donations we've received public as well.
Folks - this is when we need you most. A poll shows us at 48%, we are getting outspent, and voting happens in two weeks. So we're asking:
$3 to help us file a report with a huge number of individual donations.
$3 because a big report will send a powerful message to Republicans who want to redistrict in other states that there is a price to pay for moving forward.
$3 because voting starts in two weeks and this is money we need to raise to WIN.
When this campaign started, we didn't want to have to send a million fundraising emails. But this is what we need to win. And God help us if we don't... it will be almost impossible to take back the House for years.
[**Please contribute $3 or whatever you can afford to help Gavin Newsom pass the Election Rigging Response Act in California. Do it before tomorrow's deadline ends.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/erra-em?refcode=em250919-1&refcode2=2567_4767699_Wjqy19&akid=2567%2E4767699%2EWjqy19)

name: Donald Trump, Jr.
subject: Charlie’s legacy must live on
body:
Let’s finish what Charlie started
His fire lit a movement. It's up to us to keep it burning.

| | | [](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=d9d90a1ccf514bc7b07450633fb8eb9a&_e=fljZsLfL2UHsODt-VVkLq2UWviV6ErdXoLsivLzbX3AudjSqAtl4Jng2OOa-R9K3XdK6TNPPdZ7U8A3I9VAx7Xr69xpx-z2nUHN3-BO6n51hQwqM3Nq3PDPh15U3pQDVIKWRX4WkzDMSy_KhI5Yv6BwAUEWsgG3mzXVErRIAVIsF13SjHnNc1JiWk266LBNcal71nvDdLiSjTuH-6QlvI2F1FGMz_HiV_i5VJZNM_xraQrVT1JjjP9I5Eb_zPQrP8zXysuFr5ciD8N4BZTrl4qEMYS3CDIuAQ9OCrC8Dbh9Ulvz_W8-eUucQpqnPRViPH0tYP-Y1KWfbNO-huT4LZaMOmZv2GIkDNqpkXiSNwEOSa9WqkqIB20iaV255S_tBV1ZmyhGk0euR2UhT2XJ1VRZu1H9yPeX7Uq8fSsqbXU4WqtntGiyeDuKqXyXqD6wUuhXYuG9xz7OzsWAR-JK9wfLHrkYITH1o9TNrhHvo6vKD_MPFC7ByBPFlMadPl6V93t8cRjp6HOpgfkLptBi3jxtvlK-T-zXM10cW8LyDOS8ksC0tmLOxYSkIeoiuLcyB5-iOOyh4iOG-Yks9UyabPJNOb24BqXNZItwYMsdYda3K5ddkpc8juLmPAs_q9BSeSmvv8p8dHdKzp1ibZ7eCkoHNSOrpYNIStx7b9NwlW_Qs_5EF20pR2iYaRM7I6s5BiGD9kDF123FD7c4w6WRRRtmZ-vCsOnZUKYRMa2qaN9k%3D)
---
Peter, I don't even know how to begin to put into words the loss I am feeling... but this isn't about me. **Today, tomorrow, next week -- they're all about Charlie. ** Because Charlie was truly one of the best men I'll ever know. He changed the direction of this nation. He was a leader, a fighter, and a man whose character and conviction were rare. Too rare. He was an inspiration to millions around the world. **To me, Charlie was a little brother.** That honor I will carry with me forever.

It's up to us to keep it burning. | | | Peter, I don't even know how to begin to put into words the loss I am feeling... but this isn't about me. Today, tomorrow, next week -- they're all about Charlie. Because Charlie was truly one of the best men I'll ever know. He changed the direction of this nation. He was a leader, a fighter, and a man whose character and conviction were rare. Too rare. He was an inspiration to millions around the world. To me, Charlie was a little brother. name: Urgent Redistricting Update via Our Revolution
subject: Stop Trump’s Redistricting Power Grab – Help Pass Prop 50!

body:
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34059?t=1061&refcode=email-20250919_v&akid=9395%2E5885482%2EYN1R9Z ]Our Revolution logo

Peter Willis,

We have a huge announcement: Our Revolution is endorsing California’s Prop
50 – the Election Rigging Response Act – and we’re putting organizers on
the ground to win it.

Why? Because Donald Trump and Republicans access the country have launched
a MASSIVE national redistricting effort in Texas, Missouri, Ohio, Florida,
and more – desperately trying to steal enough seats to keep control of
Congress no matter how the people vote.

Prop 50 is our chance to fight back. By passing the Election Rigging
Response Act, California voters can ensure their voices aren’t silenced by
Republican gerrymandering – and prevent Trump and his allies from
cementing control of our government for years to come.

We’re going all in – mobilizing our grassroots base to support local
groups on the ground with canvassing, phonebanking, and voter education.
Together, we’re aiming to reach 1.2 million contacts across California
before election day.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34059?t=1107&amount=22&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&refcode=email-20250919nd&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9395%2E5885482%2EYN1R9Z ]Ballots drop in just over TWO WEEKS and we need to raise $50,000 before
then to power this work. Chip in now to defend democracy and help Our
Revolution pass Prop 50.

If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation to Our Revolution will process immediately.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34059?source=email-20250919&t=1110&akid=9395%2E5885482%2EYN1R9Z ]Yes on Prop 50 Graphic

Donald Trump and Republicans in states like Texas and Missouri are
gerrymandering congressional seats to rig the 2026 election before voting
even begins. And more Republican-led states are following suit every day.

We have to fight back with every tool available – and our best bet is
redistricting in California. But the truth is, we’re up against corporate
PAC money fueling the right-wing’s war chest. Republican billionaires will
spend whatever it takes to stop Prop 50. But if we stand together – if we
show up, organize, and outwork them – we can win.

Ballots will start hitting mailboxes on October 6. That means we have just
days to reach voters before they start to vote. We need to raise $50,000
so our teams can hire more organizers, do more canvassing, and make more
calls to reach more voters and cut through the right-wing propaganda
blitz.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34059?t=1104&amount=22&refcode=email-20250919nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9395%2E5885482%2EYN1R9Z ]The stakes could not be higher: Prop 50 is our firewall against Trump’s

sure we win?

Let’s put power back in the hands of the people.

name: John Hickenlooper
subject: I’m frustrated, and I bet you are too
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY HICKENLOOPER FOR COLORADO
body:
So here's my promise to you: In this unprecedented and critical moment, we’re doing everything we can to fight back.

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115520890/570595255/1435897075?refcode=email_091825_promise-rs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MjEyNTI1NS02Mjk1LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BpzGm1xXmawBUo1U4zDapDzbm7NxhaKQKomqgxmaY14=&emci=a0027eb9-c794-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=82125255-6295-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=7280715) Team, we’ve been talking with folks all across Colorado – at town halls, at community events, and as we go about our days – and one thing is abundantly clear: People are just as frustrated as I am, and I bet you are too. **Donald Trump and Republicans in Congress have gutted Medicaid and SNAP, they’re wreaking havoc on our economy with reckless tariff-taxes, and ripping away funding from critical services that so many Coloradans rely on.** Look, the people of Colorado sent me to Washington to stand up for you and deliver real results. **So this is my promise to you: In this unprecedented and critical moment, we’re doing everything we can to fight back.** We’re challenging Trump and extreme Republicans’ radical plans — like their plan to sell off millions of acres of Colorado’s public land — and making sure everyone understands the pain they’re causing. We’ll continue to do everything in our power to block their extreme agenda and defend working families. But we can’t do this alone. **We need to be united and create a massive grassroots effort to call out Trump’s reckless decisions and stop him from inflicting even more damage on our country.** **[So today, I’m coming to you. name: Gabby Giffords
subject: You probably don’t read a lot of pieces written jointly by a Republican and a Democrat these days.
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
Some moments are too important for partisanship.
#

Team,
Some moments are too important for partisanship. The moment our country is in today is one of them.
I just wrote a new op-ed for USA Today with former Republican U.S. Senator Jeff Flake. It talks about the leadership we need to bring us out of this dangerous moment and where we go from here. I hope you’ll take a moment out of your day to read it.
**[DONATE](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-sep25.vnrwKJ&refcode=em250919-1-b&t=1&akid=10887%2E3935704%2E_CFDmz)**

* * *
[](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-sep25.vnrwKJ&refcode=em250919-1-b&t=2&akid=10887%2E3935704%2E_CFDmz)
**We're a Republican and a Democrat. After Charlie Kirk's death, we agree on this.**
Gabby Giffords and Jeff Flake

September 2025
You probably don’t read a lot of pieces written jointly by a Republican and a Democrat these days. We served together in Congress, both representing districts in Arizona, until Gabby was nearly assassinated at an event with her constituents in 2011. When Gabby heroically returned to Congress for the 2012 State of the Union address, we bucked tradition and sat together, instead of sitting on opposite sides of the aisle with our respective parties.
Some moments are too important for partisanship. The moment our country is in today is one of them.
Since the attempt on Gabby’s life, political violence has raged across America. In 2017, a man targeted Republican Rep. Steve Scalise and others during a congressional baseball practice. In 2021, a political mob attacked police sworn to protect the U.S. Capitol while screaming to hang the vice president. One year later, a man broke into former Speaker Nancy Pelosi’s house and attacked her husband with a hammer.
Last summer, two unhinged individuals attempted to assassinate President Donald Trump. This June, an extremist shot and killed a Democratic former Speaker of the Minnesota House of Representatives, Melissa Hortman, and her husband. This gunman also wounded another lawmaker and his wife.
And on Sept. 10, an apparent sniper killed conservative activist Charlie Kirk at a public event on a Utah college campus. Jeff gave a speech on this same campus the day before Charlie Kirk was assassinated.
**Calls for 'war' and retribution have to stop now**
Already, extreme voices are attempting to hijack this debate. There are calls for “war” targeting progressive institutions and donors, and other frightening forms of collective punishment against the president’s critics. Meanwhile, there are radical individuals on the left celebrating the tragedy as an act of twisted political justice. Political violence of any kind, waged by government agents or external extremists, is a betrayal of our founding principles.
If we Americans allow ourselves to be dragged further down this path of violence and retribution, we will lose ourselves – and our country. We will abandon the pursuit of a more perfect union and descend into a frightening era where neighbors are suspicious, strangers are enemies and political opponents are threats who must be neutralized.
This dystopian future might feel good to social media warriors posting memes, but those of us who want our country to succeed know that letting ourselves be divided into extreme factions will be the end of the great American experiment of democracy.
There will always be disagreements in free societies. No matter where someone falls on the ideological spectrum, there will be people whose views they find to be wrongheaded, ignorant and morally repugnant, and sometimes the feeling is mutual.
In a democracy like ours, we all disagree sometimes, and that’s a good thing. We often disagreed when we served together, and we often learned from it. But disagreements must never become death sentences – if that happens, all of us are at risk, and so is our democracy.
**Bipartisanship is the only way forward to end gun violence**
As former members of Congress – one a Democrat and one a Republican – we believe there are two key approaches to halt the slide into violent factionalism.
First, we need sound leadership. That shouldn't be too much to ask for. Those with power and influence – from the president on down – must realize that fanning the flames of division will only make things worse and lead to even more violence and crime.
Kirk’s supporters are angry – we all are – but in this perilous moment, elected officials wield outsized influence on whether the justified anger at the assassin transforms into malice toward fellow Americans. At a moment when everyone seems on edge, irresponsible rhetoric or behavior from people in power has the potential to lead us down an even darker path.
Second, we need to talk about how astonishingly easy it is for violent extremists to assemble arsenals of guns to use against people whom social media characters and algorithms persuade them to hate. This isn’t about left or right, Democrats or Republicans. By now, we know all too well that murderous radicals of any political stripe can be a threat to anyone in the public sphere, as well as the innocent people around them.
Like the majority of Americans, we both support protecting the gun rights of responsible, law-abiding gun owners. But the stakes are too high to rely solely on the honor system – we need more effective background checks and "red flag" laws to remove guns from people who pose an imminent danger.
Decades from now, Americans will know how this generation of leaders responded in these trying moments. As former elected officials, we have learned that political careers are brief but that legacies last forever. In the wake of Charlie Kirk's horrific assassination, and following other concerning episodes of political violence in recent years, we hope politicians and commentators on all sides take a moment, put down the phone or push their chairs back from their computers, and seriously consider how all of our actions in the coming days and weeks will shape this country for future generations.
* * *

Please, take a second to read this...
As a victim of political violence, Gabby Giffords has led the fight to save more lives from gun violence for more than a decade, but she can’t do it alone.
If everyone reading this donated just $17, we'd have the resources to pass commonsense gun safety legislation across the country. But not everyone gives — making it even more important for people who care and understand the stakes of our struggle to pitch in what they can.
**If $17 seems worth it to you to change our gun laws in order to make our communities safer from gun violence, we're asking you to contribute to GIFFORDS PAC today.**
**[DONATE](https://go.giffords.org/go/61660?c0=giffords-em-sep25.Kx2kIW&refcode=em250919-1-b&t=3&akid=10887%2E3935704%2E_CFDmz)**

Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. www.Giffords.org
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. We try to send only the most important information and opportunities to participate via email, but click [here](https://go.giffords.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=10887%2E3935704%2E_CFDmz) if you'd like to unsubscribe. Giffords PAC was started to support elected officials who step up to fight the gun violence epidemic. Thank you for standing with Gabby and the Giffords team.
clean_body: Some moments are too important for partisanship. Team, Some moments are too important for partisanship. The moment our country is in today is one of them. I just wrote a new op-ed for USA Today with former Republican U.S. Senator Jeff Flake. It talks about the leadership we need to bring us out of this dangerous moment and where we go from here. I hope you’ll take a moment out of your day to read it. We're a Republican and a Democrat. After Charlie Kirk's death, we agree on this. Gabby Giffords and Jeff Flake September 2025 You probably don’t read a lot of pieces written jointly by a Republican and a Democrat these days. We served together in Congress, both representing districts in Arizona, until Gabby was nearly assassinated at an event with her constituents in 2011. When Gabby heroically returned to Congress for the 2012 State of the Union address, we bucked tradition and sat together, instead of sitting on opposite sides of the aisle with our respective parties. Some moments are too important for partisanship. The moment our country is in today is one of them. Since the attempt on Gabby’s life, political violence has raged across America. In 2017, a man targeted Republican Rep. Steve Scalise and others during a congressional baseball practice. In 2021, a political mob attacked police sworn to protect the U.S. Capitol while screaming to hang the vice president. One year later, a man broke into former Speaker Nancy Pelosi’s house and attacked her husband with a hammer. Last summer, two unhinged individuals attempted to assassinate President Donald Trump. This June, an extremist shot and killed a Democratic former Speaker of the Minnesota House of Representatives, Melissa Hortman, and her husband. This gunman also wounded another lawmaker and his wife. And on Sept. 10, an apparent sniper killed conservative activist Charlie Kirk at a public event on a Utah college campus. Jeff gave a speech on this same campus the day before Charlie Kirk was assassinated. Calls for 'war' and retribution have to stop now Already, extreme voices are attempting to hijack this debate. There are calls for “war” targeting progressive institutions and donors, and other frightening forms of collective punishment against the president’s critics. Meanwhile, there are radical individuals on the left celebrating the tragedy as an act of twisted political justice. Political violence of any kind, waged by government agents or external extremists, is a betrayal of our founding principles. If we Americans allow ourselves to be dragged further down this path of violence and retribution, we will lose ourselves – and our country. We will abandon the pursuit of a more perfect union and descend into a frightening era where neighbors are suspicious, strangers are enemies and political opponents are threats who must be neutralized. This dystopian future might feel good to social media warriors posting memes, but those of us who want our country to succeed know that letting ourselves be divided into extreme factions will be the end of the great American experiment of democracy. There will always be disagreements in free societies. No matter where someone falls on the ideological spectrum, there will be people whose views they find to be wrongheaded, ignorant and morally repugnant, and sometimes the feeling is mutual. In a democracy like ours, we all disagree sometimes, and that’s a good thing. We often disagreed when we served together, and we often learned from it. But disagreements must never become death sentences – if that happens, all of us are at risk, and so is our democracy. Bipartisanship is the only way forward to end gun violence As former members of Congress – one a Democrat and one a Republican – we believe there are two key approaches to halt the slide into violent factionalism. First, we need sound leadership. That shouldn't be too much to ask for. Like the majority of Americans, we both support protecting the gun rights of responsible, law-abiding gun owners. ---|---|---
There's an amusing trend in Idaho Republican politics that's worth discussing...
It involves Ronald Reagan's famous "Eleventh Commandment" - the one about _not speaking ill of fellow Republicans_. I've never been a fan. Because, apparently, we're only supposed to remember this sacred principle when certain elected officials face criticism.
Here's where it gets interesting.
Many of these same voices invoking Reagan have spent considerable time criticizing Idaho's current Republican Governor Brad Little. They've had plenty to say about Spencer Cox, Mitt Romney, Lindsey Graham, Liz Cheney, and Mike Johnson, whenever these Republicans stepped out of line with their preferred positions.
But, the second you call out one of their favorite personalities... they invoke the "11th commandment!"
The pattern is clear: Reagan's principle gets dusted off only when it shields favored elected officials from criticism, while conveniently disappearing when those same voices want to attack Republicans they don't like.
But principles should not be selective, right?
Reagan's guidance was about party unity, sure. But what we're seeing isn't unity - it's selective loyalty based on personal preference and personalities rather than consistent conservative principles.
When we create different standards for different Republicans, we move away from principled evaluation toward pure tribalism.
And frankly, that's exactly how we end up with the political mess we complain about.
_**Authentic conservative governance requires holding all elected officials accountable to the same standard, regardless of whether we personally like them or not.**_
Shocking concept, I know.
The goal isn't to create enemies within the party. It's to ensure that our evaluation of leaders stays grounded in actual conservative principles rather than shifting like the wind based on who's doing the talking and who's being talked about.
Reagan also said: "Trust, but verify."
Funny how selective the verification gets.

| Peter,
The situation on the ground in the obscure but critically important November, 2025, Pennsylvania Supreme Court Election is turning into exactly what we have been warning about:
A massive Lord of the Ring-esque all out pitched battle with spending that will reach levels comparable to a Presidential election.
Why is so much emphasis being placed on this local court race? Because if Democrats lose this court, we lose Pennsylvania forever. 19 electoral college votes guaranteed to Trump. A redistrict that guarantees 18 red congressional seats. And an extreme abortion ban.

Now after months of going it alone, organizations from across the Nation are coming in to back us up. All within the past 48 hours, the ACLU has come in with a massive ad buy, and the Democratic Legislative Campaign Committee has made a six figure investment into the race.
Once seen as a foregone lost cause, the Democratic comeback that we launched this Summer has shocked the nation and shown that we can win in Pennsylvania.
While Democrats are finally waking up to this race so too are MAGA mega donors like Jeff Yass. Yass is the richest man in PA and PACs and nonprofits that Yass has historically funded to the tune of millions are spending serious money in this race.

clean_body: The November, 2025, Pennsylvania Supreme Court Election is turning into exactly what we have been warning about: a massive Lord of the Ring-esque battle | | | | | == | Peter, The situation on the ground in the obscure but critically important November, 2025, Pennsylvania Supreme Court Election is turning into exactly what we have been warning about: A massive Lord of the Ring-esque all out pitched battle with spending that will reach levels comparable to a Presidential election. Why is so much emphasis being placed on this local court race? Because if Democrats lose this court, we lose Pennsylvania forever. 19 electoral college votes guaranteed to Trump. A redistrict that guarantees 18 red congressional seats. And an extreme abortion ban. | == | | | | For months, as one of the only Democratic organizations in this race, we have been outnumbered and outgunned, standing alone in the face of a seemingly unstoppable Republican onslaught. However, thanks to supporters like you who have gotten out the word about this race and supported our cause helping us to raise over $1.4 million dollars and hire hundreds of canvassers, we have shown the Democratic movement that there is a credible fight being put up. Now after months of going it alone, organizations from across the Nation are coming in to back us up. All within the past 48 hours, the ACLU has come in with a massive ad buy, and the Democratic Legislative Campaign Committee has made a six figure investment into the race. Once seen as a foregone lost cause, the Democratic comeback that we launched this Summer has shocked the nation and shown that we can win in Pennsylvania. While Democrats are finally waking up to this race so too are MAGA mega donors like Jeff Yass. Yass is the richest man in PA and PACs and nonprofits that Yass has historically funded to the tune of millions are spending serious money in this race. Earlier this year, Yass reportedly said he was prepared to spend up to $30 million to flip the PA Supreme Court. If he doubles down, we're in real trouble. == | | | | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | == | == | == | | == | == | == | | | | == This is shaping up to be a battle at the scale of Minas Tirith and Helms Deep. We only win this if the Rohirrim (you, our grassroots donors) can come to our aid. Thank you for your support when Pennsylvania needs it most. Team Defend Our Courts | | | | | | | | | | == | | | | | == | | | | Mail checks to: | | | | Defend Our CourtsP.O. This kit is 82 pieces of life-saving first-aid items, packed inside of a rugged waterproof Military bag that weighs less than 2 pounds.

**Everything from a tourniquet, large bandages, gauze, a razor sharp knife, burn gell, to gloves and medical tape.**

It's the ultimate first aid kit that instantly prepares you for any emergency.

[Read more about what's inside and why you should have one (or more)](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/21969/6374453/98a939d7a8ec529184800697c4d0f888bb8128c8/db69c613b776d295193e299b656032b7b19550d9)

Plus I'm giving it away to you for a massive discount today.

Think of it as a thank you for being a subscriber and loyal customer.

**Reveal the borderline-insane price I'm giving these away for**

But this deal isn't going to last.

People are already grabbing 5 at a time to stash in their vehicles and give away as gifts.

So if you go to enter your information in and the page says sold out, I'm sorry...

But if you're still able to pick one up, I highly suggest you do it now while you still can.

Republicans in Congress care more about tax breaks for billionaires than healthcare for our families.
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115522526/570600282/41798106?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzk3MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwOWNiY2UxZS02NTk1LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xfccO1Pbyr75xsULXkWZRiSDFqj56K2U_BHlqzEiC5U=&emci=2dec9b6b-ed93-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=09cbce1e-6595-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=26261389)

| | Right now, millions of Americans – including hundreds of thousands in New Jersey – are at risk of losing their healthcare or facing massive cost increases. And let's be clear about why: **Donald Trump and Republicans in Congress are sabotaging the Affordable Care Act.** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/115522527/570600283/1129285626?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzk3MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwOWNiY2UxZS02NTk1LWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xfccO1Pbyr75xsULXkWZRiSDFqj56K2U_BHlqzEiC5U=&emci=2dec9b6b-ed93-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=09cbce1e-6595-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=26261389) Americans' healthcare premiums are about to go up hundreds of dollars because Republicans in Congress care more about tax breaks for billionaires than healthcare for our families. **They can do something about it, but they're not.** This isn't some accident. This is part of a deliberate strategy. _Republicans couldn't repeal the Affordable Care Act, so they're chipping away at it through the back door, one policy change at a time, hoping you won't notice._ **We need to fight back.** Without action, people in New Jersey and across the country will pay more, get less, and many could lose their coverage altogether. That's unacceptable. I've fought to protect healthcare access from day one, and I'll never stop. But I need you with me. This is going to take all of us speaking out, organizing, and demanding better. clean_body: Republicans in Congress care more about tax breaks for billionaires than healthcare for our families. | | | | Right now, millions of Americans – including hundreds of thousands in New Jersey – are at risk of losing their healthcare or facing massive cost increases. And let's be clear about why: Donald Trump and Republicans in Congress are sabotaging the Affordable Care Act. Americans' healthcare premiums are about to go up hundreds of dollars because Republicans in Congress care more about tax breaks for billionaires than healthcare for our families. They can do something about it, but they're not. This isn't some accident. This is part of a deliberate strategy. Republicans couldn't repeal the Affordable Care Act, so they're chipping away at it through the back door, one policy change at a time, hoping you won't notice. We need to fight back. Without action, people in New Jersey and across the country will pay more, get less, and many could lose their coverage altogether. That's unacceptable. I've fought to protect healthcare access from day one, and I'll never stop. But I need you with me. This is going to take all of us speaking out, organizing, and demanding better. name: The Democratic National Committee
subject: Here’s what a well-timed donation can do:
body:
---
Derek, We know your inbox is probably stuffed with fundraising requests, but we hope you’ll give us a chance to explain why a well-timed donation to the Democratic National Committee is the best choice if you’re looking to make a difference right now. **Voters are beginning to cast ballots in states like Virginia, and we’re running out of time to reach those who may still be undecided.**[**We’ll explain more about what our strategy looks like in a second, but if you’re ready to donate to the DNC now, you can do that at this link.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-september-2025?refcode=em_20250919_4609_fr&amount=5&refcode2=4609_22930333_dxL3nC&akid=4609%2E22930333%2EdxL3nC) Here’s the truth: _Everything_ we do right now makes a difference in whether or not we can reach our goals — not just to win elections this year, but to flip Congress and elect leaders who will check Trump’s power at every level. That work takes a HUGE number of resources — resources we need to gather _now_. So far this year...
* Democrats have won or overperformed the top of the 2024 ticket in 43 out of 44 key elections.
* The DNC has continued to make investments in every single state Democratic Party to ensure they have the resources to elect local and state-level leaders.
* We’ve made seven-figure investments in Virginia and New Jersey ahead of this year’s statewide elections.
* We deployed tens of thousands of volunteers over the summer nationwide to activate, mobilize, and prepare voters to engage in their next elections, and we’re continuing to build on that momentum this fall.

**This work has only been possible this year because we’ve been cultivating resources early.** Grassroots supporters are the backbone of our work, getting Democrats elected up and down the ballot and pushing back against Trump and Republicans across the country. [**So, Derek, can Democrats everywhere count on you to make a well-timed $5 donation to the DNC today? We’ll put every dollar to work building the resources we’ll need to elect Democrats this year and beyond.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-september-2025?refcode=em_20250919_4609_fr&amount=5&refcode2=4609_22930333_dxL3nC&akid=4609%2E22930333%2EdxL3nC) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $5](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-september-2025?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=em_20250919_4609_fr&refcode2=4609_22930333_dxL3nC&akid=4609%2E22930333%2EdxL3nC)**

# [Hey Republicans, do something to save your country, you cowards!](https://default.salsalabs.org/Ta4e07a58-61c5-4cd7-90bc-8af1383de5a1/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T94bd4368-90c1-431c-a4eb-ad20d409cae9/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | [**> > Add your name: "Hey Republicans, do something to save your country, you cowards!"**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T3be5dfe1-193a-41e6-829b-4449631f8acd/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) It's time to stop giving Republicans a free pass just because we've grown to expect them to be horrible people, and too chicken to stand up to Trump. **They built this mess, now they've got to get off their asses, and clean it up.** Republicans put Trump in the White House, and they stood by and did nothing while he tore apart the country. And then they put him in AGAIN, and are doing nothing while he tears up the Constitution, and destroys America. If even a few Republicans in Congress stood up to Trump, they could join with Democrats to block him on a wide range of critical fronts. They could even start with the [Epstein files, where 4 Republicans have already signed a discharge petition to force a vote to release them](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tf2d38867-b2ba-48af-8462-1e6e2e6ca4ff/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)). They took an oath to defend the constitution--now they need to do it. To hell with the excuses. They need to save their country. They need to rein in this wannabe dictator. And they need to start now. [**Sign on: "Hey Republicans do something to save your country, you cowards." **](https://default.salsalabs.org/T373f5988-b37e-45a1-b49f-de44a00494be/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

Nevada is as battleground as battlegrounds get.
* Cook Political Report listed Joe Lombardo as _the most vulnerable_ Republican Governor up for election.
* The NRCC put all three Democratic House members on their top targets list.
* The share of nonpartisan voters in Nevada is growing rapidly and they need to hear from us.

Nothing about this current term is normal and Donald Trump is already meddling in unprecedented ways to hold onto power, including biased gerrymandering in red states.
**We have to be prepared to fight back against every cheap play Trump and his MAGA donors will throw our way. That’s why,** **[I’m asking for your support now before the end of quarter so that NV Dems has the resources to win.](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqu4ksFa5SF7IXH6Sr7Lz9yTaFWLw6zUtEkIcUI4H1v3OdJrJXNQTLUDXBDpio_uFqR5vClzCCYBrLnIJQZUWFVrjp2yvr1WGXm-TKdVpripkBXrmIIo4AD_si7SAMIvpsDi4Zs2vYJbkzaHg2Oow0qNrFA2_p9YpZnXOc1R84fjmNMlzlcrNuuqYlMC5LSfOYV9kMB43zvkkR3l2QnjtlBNF7a6B0YaQgkGz4r6J-_zgd21ETsypQmniE11rO8gb9gV2kOqxMeYEjHsrMakDwPpw6XhStWIsglCI7LZtnPSRmi8GGra3fwzJmh0JeVKfxjdVCt_wnuGFZ-lD2DoX1hq/4k1/QE1l65HZSQOatqg9rQakog/h2/h001.PURwa9GtUFUHEalIjhXw0b9gChYGSFggWZzFQRWLFwk)**

name: Juan Ciscomani
subject: We have lost a true Patriot
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CISCOMANI FOR CONGRESS
body:
|
---
| | [ ](https://juanciscomani.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4ed32a393c9fe1089f10fee94&id=7a9ba3b1bb&e=659fba240e)
---
| Laura and I join millions of Americans in mourning the horrific loss of Charlie Kirk. Charlie leaves behind a legacy of love of God, love of family, and love of country. He lit a fire that brought so many young Americans along with him, and his voice for conservative values echoed far beyond Arizona.

This assassination was sheer evil. Political violence has no place in our country. As a nation, we must be better than this—and it must end.

Charlie believed in free speech and lived that belief by example. In these divided times, we can all learn from his commitment to engagement over division.

Laura and I are praying for Erika, their two young children, his friends, the entire Turning Point family, and all who were touched by his work. They have lost a loving husband and father, and we have lost a Patriot the likes of which we will not see again.

Prayerfully,
Juan

body:
There is nothing — absolutely nothing — that will divert my attention from the needs of Virginia families.
Peter, Donald Trump attacked me from the Oval Office.
_But I will not be rattled._
I will not be intimidated by somebody who's fired thousands of Virginians, driven up costs, and closed our rural health clinics. _In fact, let the President come down to Virginia and answer for the chaos he's unleashed._
There is nothing — absolutely nothing — that will divert my attention from the needs of Virginia families.

**High school dropout. Waffle House waitress. First woman to graduate from The Citadel. Single mom. Fighter.** **Nancy Mace wasn’t born into power. She worked for every inch of ground she’s gained.** At 17, she dropped out of high school and took a job slinging hash browns at Waffle House to survive. No silver spoon. No Ivy League connections. Just grit, grace, and a whole lot of graveyard shifts. **By the grace of God, she turned that struggle into a story no politician can touch.** She made history as the first woman to graduate from The Citadel, South Carolina’s legendary military college. She built her own business. She raised two kids on her own. And when the Left came for everything she believed in, she stood her ground.Now, she’s running for Governor of South Carolina. Nancy Mace is a battle-tested conservative who’s walked through fire and come out swinging. In Congress, she’s been one of President Trump’s fiercest allies, fighting to secure our border, protect our daughters, and stop the Left’s sick agenda dead in its tracks. **She’s not here to make friends. She’s here to make history.** Nancy is fighting to:
* Eliminate South Carolina’s Income Tax—the highest in the Southeast
* Prosecute violent criminals and pedophiles with zero leniency
* Crack down on sanctuary sheriffs who let illegal aliens roam free
* Defund radical public universities that indoctrinate, not educate
* Reform education so students learn how to think—not what to think
* Work hand-in-hand with President Trump to put South Carolina FIRST

Through every hardship, Nancy never gave up. And now she’s ready to do what she’s always done: roll up her sleeves, take the heat, and lead from the front. **South Carolina is worth fighting for. And Nancy Mace is just getting started.**
---
| |
# PAID FOR BY NANCY MACE FOR GOVERNOR

| | 295 Seven Farms Drive, Suite C-186Charleston, SC 29492

body:
| | | [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtHtWNDtXR0WReOmqZcCOXOeD5Hv8jrBs2gR4cymg2NP762eOOvHiIFZT4pM11kYG3ULpFFl-PvHmAYtj78MzaUufRxowTJd3-MuHEbobxS9a6VKQcTfU2xgzz5HWD1lIIbCzviPKEaPW2NHUgbeRGoEipd6boDRzdmPjVyxUFbUcLR-32jtfoM2tG-xDwYr32sZdJhMiPMYQdQfPN3dlrUjdBGyxlM_h3TSHS5qV7HioSxbXpEz8ode-AS6Y9womdISyYvPBZe9sXayrzH267sboyMIG0Q1WRY5GLi-kOu8urI3PwY5buGVStqWbZ3pciRhzrkV0chj3ewFDZiCxzRZHS8wTR2544ysm8LlVBfLg/4k1/d2cSbvctT8y7gZCDML0b6Q/h0/h001.DFi-8ohSK4F9cgMZIyt8uu8XXy78DtQs1GZUTrXJfr8) **** Hey,Friend, my name is Josh Turek and I’m the Democrat who’s going to replace Joni Ernst in Iowa’s must-win Senate race.
_Can I tell you a story?_
When I launched my campaign for State House in 2022, no one thought I’d be able to win in a district Trump won handily twice.
However, Friend, as a Paralympian and former pro athlete, **_I know if there’s a will, there’s a way_**. So, I hit the streets of Council Bluffs and Carter Lake to make my case to voters.
**The funny thing about my district is, unlike most of Iowa, this neck of the woods is very hilly. Most houses have 10 or 20 stairs leading up to the front porch.**
Still, I spent four months knocking on damn near every door in town. Every time someone answered, they’d ask the same question: _“How in the world did you get up here to my door?”_
[](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtHtWNDtXR0WReOmqZcCOXOeD5Hv8jrBs2gR4cymg2NPx5mFJIHAN33H-YB5ApqYP-NvDAIYLlt4_tlSy9ZKeTNx7GAesRuMmtfXcpG9V56qbqpbTMjlj8vbRq8EEOi0o04AiuMNh4dW1Tp1D6RbVe3bX87wYwBmW8unUkBuJi0ZWcKdmeDhO1eeALoVEtBW4EdKN-26VWC4oQCHmBT5yuvTqTJ9daH14-n9L-MfLHgS6Y3lo29uUJ7oHTtaLlx_YOzHdThXvaige90bVan_PWQNEl6TreZAa9TgMsBk5fQ2CLT2EAvHq0eCvMqrzyyHAcceFXNJfrZpNEhT511M50D56BhrRDfAoq8sVvos0dfKQ/4k1/d2cSbvctT8y7gZCDML0b6Q/h1/h001.p-OuP0kBi6tIo97d4XBw832CJReZrrfUiee5TrvdnLE)
| [SPLIT A DONATION](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtHtWNDtXR0WReOmqZcCOXOeD5Hv8jrBs2gR4cymg2NP3KCIcQXxUxD5jzb8Yu1dXkd9ZZ7ZzKypFUYu9zo_Z6qrazj9BqZ7qRsVdap2jG68EIiRIyDTumiiimglFRSumKX4w4sAqqn_IwCR2jHdi-BZqUH96Utg2RQa0M-bbOxwxeLCKoFg8oR1qJ4hL5AVaMJpEoLf5F9ugn1rhKJCdXeDh9wIKAuwLBUZRCtcSZ_zjzfmhNPzjW9oWH5LFxm8tC_AuZ3JURpICf-5F5o8XQvfEtkmx4JawQ42j-UAROrrTRR4hkqnEHL8kMMsE0mpBAB7nx28RgkcUbra2wWqz4UCc_xyNzWp8U_BvOmzgrSZA/4k1/d2cSbvctT8y7gZCDML0b6Q/h2/h001.KJ1FJXbZmCShJV3ztosURFxDjt0rqdQaLm5pEYGYvOw)

**I told them the truth: I crawled up here, one step at a time.** That’s how important this conversation is to me.

Friend, all that sweat and determination paid off on Election Day, when I won by six votes.

Then, in 2024, I turned my six-vote victory into a six-point victory.

That grit and grind is how we’re going to win in 2026. It won’t be easy — Iowa is one of the most competitive Senate seats in the country, according to experts — but we’re going to win this one step at a time.

One grassroots donation at a time. One county at a time. One voter at a time.

The first step here is showing grassroots strength ahead of our September 30th FEC fundraising deadline. **With your help, I’ll win this open seat and conquer the steep climb up to Capitol Hill.**

clean_body: | | | ** Hey,Friend, my name is Josh Turek and I’m the Democrat who’s going to replace Joni Ernst in Iowa’s must-win Senate race. Can I tell you a story? When I launched my campaign for State House in 2022, no one thought I’d be able to win in a district Trump won handily twice. However, Friend, as a Paralympian and former pro athlete, I know if there’s a will, there’s a way. So, I hit the streets of Council Bluffs and Carter Lake to make my case to voters. The funny thing about my district is, unlike most of Iowa, this neck of the woods is very hilly. Most houses have 10 or 20 stairs leading up to the front porch. Still, I spent four months knocking on damn near every door in town. Every time someone answered, they’d ask the same question: “How in the world did you get up here to my door?” | I told them the truth: I crawled up here, one step at a time. That’s how important this conversation is to me. Friend, all that sweat and determination paid off on Election Day, when I won by six votes. Then, in 2024, I turned my six-vote victory into a six-point victory. That grit and grind is how we’re going to win in 2026. It won’t be easy — Iowa is one of the most competitive Senate seats in the country, according to experts — but we’re going to win this one step at a time. One grassroots donation at a time. One county at a time. One voter at a time. The first step here is showing grassroots strength ahead of our September 30th FEC fundraising deadline. With your help, I’ll win this open seat and conquer the steep climb up to Capitol Hill. ** We have a ton of work to do and history to make here in Iowa. Let’s do this! Talk soon, Josh Turek elects Democrats with disabilities. We fight to protect accessible voting, expand health care and Social Security, and equality for all. To donate by check, send to: Able Dems PAC c/o Pocketbook Strategies 1110 N Virgil Ave #375 Los Angeles, CA 90029 \- \- Paid for by Able Dems PAC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. body:
News, events, and action items to keep Nebraska red and growing strong.
| | | | |
---
| Letter from the Chair
---
The Role of the Nebraska Republican Party (NEGOP)
The Nebraska Republican Party (NEGOP) is often asked, "What do you do?" While candidates are visibly campaigning--knocking on doors, speaking to voters, and raising funds with the urgency of an election deadline--the NEGOP operates tirelessly, year-round, to provide the essential framework that supports Republican candidates and elected officials. As the "middle man" in the party structure, the NEGOP bridges the gap between national leadership, state operations, and grassroots efforts.
Our Role in the Republican Ecosystem At the top, President Donald J. Trump and Vice President JD Vance serve as the titular heads of the Republican Party, shaping its overall direction. The Republican National Committee (RNC) supports state parties like the NEGOP, which, in turn, empowers county parties. This interconnected structure ensures a unified effort to advance Republican values and elect candidates at all levels. The NEGOP is the backbone of this system in Nebraska, providing resources, strategy, and coordination to strengthen Republican campaigns. Unlike candidates with finite campaign cycles, our work is continuous, laying the groundwork for success in every election.
Preparing for the 2026 Election Cycle As we head into the fall of 2025, the NEGOP is focused on preparing for the 2026 election cycle. Our efforts include:
* Recruiting Candidates: Identifying and supporting strong Republican candidates for local, state, and federal offices.
* Fundraising: Securing the financial resources necessary to fuel effective campaigns.
* Strategic Planning: Developing comprehensive plans to maximize support for all GOP candidates.
* Voter Engagement: Driving voter registration and Get-Out-the-Vote (GOTV) initiatives to boost turnout.
* Campaign Resources: Providing campaigns with free tools, such as walk apps and data access, to enhance outreach and efficiency.

These efforts are designed to create a cohesive, well-supported network of Republican candidates who can compete and win.
The Power of County Parties County parties are the grassroots heart of the NEGOP. They play a critical role by:
* Organizing local Republicans to build community and momentum.
* Recruiting candidates for local races, ensuring strong representation at every level.
* Serving as a vital feedback loop, relaying the concerns and priorities of Nebraska voters to the state party.

Unity is Our Strength When the NEGOP, county parties, and candidates work together, we achieve our greatest success. Collaboration creates economies of scale, fosters synergy, and amplifies our impact, leading to more Republican victories. However, if we operate in silos, we risk inefficiency and missed opportunities. By uniting our efforts, sharing resources, and aligning our goals, the NEGOP ensures that Nebraska Republicans are positioned to win elections and advance our shared values. Together, we build a stronger, more effective party for the future.

Mary Jane Truemper Chairman, Nebraska Republican Party
---
| |
